mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 cash flows and generating positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 20-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, with a negative or zero Rank IC observed in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls indicate sustained economic growth and rising household income, which supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive power for 5-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple market cycles, or returns are negative in > 60% of positive payroll months.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 320.0, persistent inflation erodes real corporate earnings and forces monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 320.0, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales validates robust aggregate demand, boosting earnings forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth signal yields negative 20-day returns in > 55% of occurrences, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample predictive modeling.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion signal supply chain bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing output, which compresses profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle expansions fail to predict negative 5-day returns, with a positive Rank IC observed in walk-forward tests, or the signal loses predictive accuracy (p > 0.10).
